title: Data updates policy | Stats NZ
content:
02 February 2022, 9:56am
The Government Statistician is responsible and accountable for the release of data, statistics, insights, and other information produced by Stats NZ (collectively referred to as releases).
This data updates policy guides how we manage updates to Stats NZ data and statistics in our releases.
A data update is a change to a previously released statistic; Stats NZ does this when:
- new or more timely information has become available
- new methods or data sources are introduced
- existing methods or data sources are updated.
A data update differs from an error in that it is not due to a mistake, rather it is an improvement to previously published figures by incorporating the most up-to-date methods, data, and data sources available.
Stats NZ updates its data, statistics, insights, and information to improve its quality. Stats NZ does this so that our treaty partners and customers, stakeholders, and commentators are well informed and best able to gain value from the latest data available.
The following principles must be applied when considering the appropriate practice for updates.
- Transparency – Updates are clearly visible, are communicated and disseminated openly, and should be signalled in advance. Stats NZ will ensure our treaty partners are engaged with and well informed.
- Accuracy – Updates should exhibit a high degree of statistical integrity.
- Equity and equality – Updates should be as accessible and freely available to users as per the original releases. Appropriate explanation and/or documentation should accompany the updates.
- Impact – Updates should be proportionate and material, with consideration for any impact on data users and the data system, whilst maintaining the independence of the Government Statistician.
- Release practices policy
This policy and associated procedures and standards are guided by:
- Data and Statistics Act 2022
- United Nations Fundamental Principles of Official Statistics
- The Principles and Protocols for Producers of Tier 1 Statistics
- The Privacy Act 2020
- The Public Service Act 2020
- Mana Ōrite Relationship Agreement
Policy owner: General Manager, Statistical Change and Assurance
Approved by: Rachael Milicich, Deputy Chief Executive/Deputy Government Statistician, Insights & Statistics
Approval date: March 2022
Date of next review: March 2024
